Name: curvature_aware_precision_routing
Title: Curvature-Aware Precision Routing: Training-Free Adaptive Mixed-Precision Optimization via Geometric Subspace Diagnostics
Short Hypothesis: Precision routing can be optimized by leveraging curvature-aware geometric subspace diagnostics, which provide formalized stability radii and PAC-Bayes generalization bounds, leading to more efficient and stable mixed-precision configurations without requiring additional training.
Related Work: Recent work in mixed-precision optimization, such as EMQ, SNIP, and AMP-ViT, has focused on training-free proxies and heuristic-based methods. However, these approaches often lack formalized stability guarantees and explicit curvature awareness. Our proposal distinguishes itself by integrating curvature-aware diagnostics, such as gradient covariance decay and Hessian conditioning, into the precision routing framework, providing rigorous theoretical bounds and deterministic execution pathways.
Abstract: Mixed-precision optimization is crucial for efficient model deployment, yet current methods often rely on heuristic-based proxies or additional training, leading to suboptimal configurations and instability. We propose Curvature-Aware Precision Routing (CAPR), a training-free framework that leverages geometric subspace diagnostics to inform precision routing. CAPR integrates gradient covariance decay, Hessian conditioning, and feature entanglement metrics to provide formalized stability radii and PAC-Bayes generalization bounds. This approach replaces heuristic fragility claims with deterministic, algorithmically grounded optimization proxies, ensuring efficient and stable mixed-precision configurations. We validate CAPR through rigorous empirical evaluation, demonstrating significant improvements in accuracy-complexity trade-offs and computational efficiency.
Experiments: Experiment 1: Diagnose gradient covariance decay and Hessian conditioning across different precision configurations using pre-execution diagnostic loops.
Experiment 2: Implement CAPR on ResNet and ViT models, comparing mixed-precision configurations derived from curvature-aware diagnostics against heuristic-based methods.
Experiment 3: Evaluate the stability and generalization performance of CAPR using formalized stability radii and PAC-Bayes generalization bounds.
Experiment 4: Conduct ablation studies to isolate the impact of individual curvature-aware diagnostics on precision routing efficiency.
Risk Factors and Limitations: Potential computational overhead from curvature-aware diagnostics may offset efficiency gains.
The effectiveness of CAPR may vary across different model architectures and datasets.
Formalized stability radii and PAC-Bayes bounds may not fully capture real-world deployment constraints.
